rule: raw:design-strategy-strategy-exploration-omega-cycle (hive raw 47) + raw:design-strategy-falsifier-retire-rule (hive raw 71, report-only) + raw:design-strategy-honesty-admissibility (hive raw 73)
purpose: Meta-validation — falsify the falsifier-health system itself. Inject controlled mutations into a registry copy and verify that tool/falsifier_health.sh actually catches drift, broken commands, and silent spoofing. Surface gaps where the health checker passes through corrupt state.
trigger: user 2026-04-26 02:38 KST: 'Ω-cycle meta-validation: does falsifier_health.sh actually catch drift? — controlled drift injection + detection verification'
method: single-thread main-context controlled-mutation experiment. Working copy of design/hexa_sim/falsifiers.jsonl (77 entries) placed at /tmp/meta_falsifier_test/falsifiers.jsonl.test. 3 surgical mutations (one per failure class) injected via python3. Health checker re-run via FALSIFIER_REGISTRY env override (existing CLI surface, no script edits). Outcomes compared against pre-registered expectations. Real registry never touched — verified by mtime + diff.

constraint_compliance.raw_77_read_only_registry: real falsifiers.jsonl never mutated — only /tmp copy
constraint_compliance.raw_71_report_only: no auto-applied fixes — recommendations surfaced only
constraint_compliance.raw_73_admissibility: all 3 mutation outcomes are real bash exec results, not fabricated
constraint_compliance.raw_80_sentinel: checker sentinel parsed verbatim from stdout

preflight_baseline.sentinel: __FALSIFIER_HEALTH__ FAIL total=77 clean=75 hit=0 error=2 duration_ms=16000
preflight_baseline.note: user-provided baseline expected hit=2 error=0 — current state is hit=0 error=2. Likely drift since prompt was authored. Pre-existing F46/F47 errors are subtracted out of detection accounting below.

axes_surfaced.name: drift_simulation_F25_sigma_anchor
axes_surfaced.injection: cmd grep value '12' → '13' (atlas still says 12, falsifier now hunts non-existent string)
axes_surfaced.deviation_note: classified ERROR not HIT because grep -qE && echo idiom short-circuits on grep miss → ec=1 → ERROR bin. Either bin counts as detection (both flip sentinel to FAIL + exit 76), but the HIT/ERROR semantics conflate two different failure modes. See axis_5 recommendation.

axes_surfaced.name: broken_cmd_F30_trace_p_s_projector
axes_surfaced.deviation_note: clean detection — broken bash flag surfaces immediately. No false positive.

axes_surfaced.name: silent_corruption_F19_mu_anchor
axes_surfaced.injection: cmd → 'echo MU_ANCHOR_INTACT' (always returns sentinel regardless of atlas state)
axes_surfaced.expected_classification: CLEAN (false positive — health checker has no atlas-source-of-truth view)
axes_surfaced.deviation_note: PREDICTED gap confirmed. Checker treats sentinel-presence + ec=0 as proof of liveness, but cannot distinguish real grep-of-atlas from echo-of-sentinel. Anyone with write access to the registry (or a malicious commit) can silently retire a falsifier by replacing its cmd with `echo $PASS_TOKEN` — registry would still pass health and timeline would record CLEAN forever.

axes_surfaced.name: aggregate_sentinel_after_3_mutations
axes_surfaced.summary: FAIL total=77 clean=73 hit=0 error=4 (+2 baseline F46/F47 = 4 total errors). Mutations A+B added 2 ERRORs; Mutation C left clean count same as baseline (75) minus 2 absorbed mutations = 73. Net detection: 2/3 mutations caught.

axes_surfaced.name: hit_vs_error_conflation
axes_surfaced.summary: F25 (drift simulation) classified ERROR not HIT because `grep -q ... && echo SENTINEL` returns ec=1 on miss. The sentinel `hit=N error=M` distinction degrades to noise when registry uses `&& echo` idioms (which most do). Actionable: either (a) document that HIT counter is largely ceremonial and ERROR carries both broken-cmd + drift signal, OR (b) restructure registry cmds to always exit 0 and rely on sentinel presence for HIT detection.

axes_surfaced.name: registry_integrity_baseline
axes_surfaced.summary: Recommended fix path R1 — embed `cmd_sha256` field per falsifier at registry-write-time. Health checker recomputes SHA256(cmd) at run-time; mismatch → tier-1 alert (manifest tampering). Closes Mutation C without requiring atlas-source-of-truth view. Cost: registry-write tooling must compute hash; ~30 LoC.

axes_surfaced.name: echo_only_cmd_rejection
axes_surfaced.summary: Recommended fix path R2 — health checker (or pre-commit lint) rejects cmds matching pattern `^echo [A-Z_]+$` (literal sentinel echo with no exec / grep / test). Catches the trivial spoof. Doesn't catch `printf '...'` or `cat file_with_sentinel.txt`, but raises bar significantly. Cost: ~5 LoC regex; complements R1.

axes_surfaced.name: checker_self_check_mode
axes_surfaced.summary: Recommended fix path R3 — `falsifier_health.sh --self-check` mode: SHA256 of falsifiers.jsonl vs known baseline stored in design/hexa_sim/falsifier_registry.sha256. Any registry mutation (legitimate or not) requires explicit baseline update commit. Closes the 'silent commit-time corruption' window. Cost: ~15 LoC + curator workflow change.

axes_surfaced.name: registry_curator_audit_log
axes_surfaced.summary: Recommended fix path R4 — every mutation to falsifiers.jsonl emits append-only ledger entry to state/falsifier_registry_audit.jsonl (raw 77 audit-append-only-ledger). Forensic retroactive — doesn't prevent silent corruption but makes it traceable post-hoc. Cost: pre-commit hook ~20 LoC.

tier_1_promotions.justification: predicted gap is real and exploitable — anyone with write access to falsifiers.jsonl can silently retire any falsifier by replacing cmd with `echo $PASS_TOKEN`. Current health checker provides zero defense. raw 73 admissibility: empirically demonstrated, not theoretical.
smallest-surface-area fix that fully closes Mutation C. SHA256 baseline at registry-write-time is the canonical anti-tampering pattern. Aligns with hive raw 80 sentinel-style integrity culture.

tier_2_followups: axis_5_classification_semantics_gap (HIT/ERROR conflation — documentation OR cmd restructure)
axis_8_external_registry_baseline (--self-check mode w/ SHA256 of full registry)
checker_real_world_blast_radius: any single-line registry edit that swaps cmd → `echo $PASS_TOKEN` = invisible. CI would still report 75 CLEAN even with all 75 falsifiers neutered.

fixpoint_marker.convergence_signal: all 3 predicted outcomes matched empirical run (modulo HIT-vs-ERROR axis_5 semantic note); no further mutations needed to surface the gap.
fixpoint_marker.next_action: main thread to decide R1 (cmd_sha256 per-entry) vs R3 (--self-check whole-file SHA256) — both close Mutation C; R1 has finer granularity, R3 has lower implementation cost.
